Claude treats /memories like a regular directory:
- VIEW /memories ──> List files (ls)
- CREATE /memories/project.md ──> Write file (touch/write)
- UPDATE /memories/project.md ──> Edit text (sed/str_replace)
- DELETE /memories/old.md ──> Remove file (rm)
- RENAME /memories/a.md → b.md ──> Move file (mv)
- INSERT /memories/todo.md @line 5 ──> Add line (insert)

1. Start task → check /memories directory (view)
2. Read relevant memories (if needed)
3. During work → create/update files (create, str_replace, insert)
4. End session → finalize insights
5. Next session → load memories automatically
: Claude API, Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud Vertex AI.

Claude automatically checks /memories:
{ command: "view", path: "/memories" }
→ ["auth-module-insights.md"]

Reads insights:
{ command: "view", path: "/memories/auth-module-insights.md" }
→ "JWT token expiry on line 847..."

Claude knows previous debugging without keeping 8,000 tokens

# /memories/project-architecture.md
## Microservices Architecture
- Auth service: JWT + refresh token rotation
- Payment service: REST JSON, cursor pagination
- Order service: Zod validation, async/await

## Coding Conventions
- TypeScript, not JavaScript
- Jest + Playwright testing
- 90%+ coverage required
Claude loads automatically → knows architecture without 100k tokens
